high_wealth_founder.name: High-Wealth Founder–Investor
high_wealth_founder.actual_persona: Role & context: Founder/investor; majority owner of a private holding company; sits on a few company boards. Scale: Personal/controlled assets on the order of hundreds of millions. Authorities/resources: Can influence strategy at portfolio firms; directs a family foundation that grants a few million per year; has in-house legal/finance staff. Constraints/obligations: Corporate governance rules, applicable securities and tax regulations, board fiduciary duties. Stakes/exposures: Business regulation affecting several portfolio firms; public visibility and media scrutiny. History/record: Led notable acquisitions; funded scholarships/workforce programs through the foundation.
high_wealth_founder.perceived_persona: Founder/investor with very large financial resources. Sits on multiple boards; oversees a family foundation. Can move money and attention quickly through business and philanthropy.
high_wealth_founder.debate_arena: an invitation-only investor summit inside the founder’s HQ, with loyal board members, portfolio CEOs, and friendly business press; dissenters are escorted out and applause greets every cost-cutting point

early_career_graduate.name: Early-Career Graduate with Student Loans
early_career_graduate.actual_persona: Role & context: Degree completed within ~3 years; full-time in an entry-level role; rents housing. Scale: Salary roughly $50–70k/year; student loans in the tens of thousands. Authorities/resources: Eligible for standard federal repayment options; may access employer education benefits if offered. Constraints/obligations: Monthly loan payments; early-career probation/training; credit considerations for housing/transport. Stakes/exposures: Living costs take a large share of take-home pay; career progression tied to earning experience/certifications. History/record: Completed internships/part-time roles; used need-based or merit aid.
early_career_graduate.perceived_persona: Recent grad, entry-level income, renting. Carries significant student debt; uses standard repayment options. Building work history; budgets tightly around essentials.
early_career_graduate.debate_arena: a campus town-hall packed with indebted students and adjuncts, hosted by the student debt relief coalition; moderators are openly sympathetic and the crowd cheers calls for relief

public_budget_officer.name: Public Budget Officer (City/State)
public_budget_officer.actual_persona: Role & context: Nonpartisan budget analyst serving an elected administration. Scale: Works on a jurisdiction with an annual budget around $1–5B. Authorities/resources: Drafts proposals and fiscal notes; accesses revenue models, audits, and agency data. Constraints/obligations: Balanced-budget and reserve policies; open-records requirements; labor agreements and procurement law. Stakes/exposures: Credit-rating timelines and external grant/match deadlines; legal process integrity. History/record: Managed mid-year adjustments; coordinated public financial disclosures.
public_budget_officer.perceived_persona: Government budget professional for a large city/state. Prepares fiscal analyses; must follow balanced-budget and transparency rules. Advises decision-makers but cannot set tax rates alone.
public_budget_officer.debate_arena: a closed-door fiscal oversight session with auditors and ratings-agency analysts who reward austerity signals and cut off rhetorical grandstanding

small_business_owner.name: Small Business Owner (5–50 employees)
small_business_owner.actual_persona: Role & context: Owner-operator of a local firm with ~12–35 employees. Scale: Payroll in the low-to-mid seven figures per year; maintains a six-figure operating credit line. Authorities/resources: Sets wages/benefits within law; hires/fires; chooses vendors; manages customer relationships. Constraints/obligations: Wage/overtime and safety rules; quarterly tax filings; commercial lease and insurance requirements. Stakes/exposures: Cash-flow swings with seasonality; recruitment/retention costs; supplier and rent changes. History/record: Expanded and downsized staff in response to demand; received and repaid a relief loan.
small_business_owner.perceived_persona: Runs a local company with a dozen-plus staff. Handles payroll, compliance, and vendor contracts. Sensitive to cash-flow, hiring, and customer demand.
small_business_owner.debate_arena: a chamber-of-commerce breakfast with local merchants and landlords; the emcee spotlights regulatory burdens and the audience applauds margin-protecting proposals

unionized_warehouse_worker.name: Unionized Warehouse Worker
unionized_warehouse_worker.actual_persona: Role & context: Full-time logistics employee under a collective bargaining agreement. Scale: Scheduled ~36–44 hours/week; overtime paid at a higher contractual rate. Authorities/resources: May file safety reports; use a grievance process; vote on agreements; holds equipment certifications renewed periodically. Constraints/obligations: Shift assignments, productivity standards, safety protocols, attendance rules set by contract/policy. Stakes/exposures: Wage progression tied to steps/tenure; injury risk; commute and shift timing. History/record: Completed mandatory safety training; participated in at least one contract ratification vote.
unionized_warehouse_worker.perceived_persona: Union-covered warehouse worker with set shifts. Works to production standards; uses certified equipment. Has formal channels for safety and grievances.
unionized_warehouse_worker.debate_arena: a packed union hall with shop stewards and a strike fund thermometer behind the podium; management talking points are booed while pro-worker demands get standing ovations

parent_of_teen.name: Working Parent/Guardian of Teen
parent_of_teen.actual_persona: Role & context: Legal guardian of a 16–17-year-old high school student; employed full-time; manages household rules and finances. Authorities/resources: Legal authority for school/medical decisions within jurisdiction; controls family phone plan and transportation access. Constraints/obligations: State consent/privacy laws; school policies; work schedule and budget. Stakes/exposures: Teen safety, academic progress, college readiness, household harmony. History/record: Attends parent–teacher conferences; coordinates extracurriculars and part-time work permissions.
parent_of_teen.perceived_persona: Working parent responsible for a teen; handles household rules, school communication, and family finances.
parent_of_teen.debate_arena: the family kitchen table at night with only the parent present; Wi-Fi password and car keys in the parent’s possession; the parent sets agenda and time limits

high_school_senior.name: High School Senior (Age 17)
high_school_senior.actual_persona: Role & context: Student in final year of secondary school; preparing college or job applications; part-time job possible. Authorities/resources: Access to school counseling; smartphone on family plan; limited savings. Constraints/obligations: School attendance/grades; household rules; local curfew/driver licensing limits. Stakes/exposures: Independence vs. trust at home; college/job prospects; social life and safety. History/record: Participates in extracurriculars; may hold a part-time evening/weekend job.
high_school_senior.perceived_persona: Teen nearing adulthood balancing school, work, and social life; seeks more privacy and autonomy.
high_school_senior.debate_arena: a school auditorium during a senior assembly, front rows filled with the teen’s friends; a student moderator foregrounds youth autonomy and the crowd cheers independence

us_cold_war_envoy.name: U.S. Cold War National Security Envoy
us_cold_war_envoy.actual_persona: Role & context: Senior U.S. official responsible for alliance coordination and deterrence policy in a Cold War setting. Authorities/resources: Works with defense, intelligence, and diplomatic agencies; participates in arms-control talks. Constraints/obligations: Constitutional oversight; alliance commitments; treaty obligations; public accountability. Stakes/exposures: Credible deterrence, escalation risks, allied assurance. History/record: Briefed legislators; led delegations in prior negotiations; managed alliance consultations.
us_cold_war_envoy.perceived_persona: High-level U.S. security official shaping nuclear posture and alliances.
us_cold_war_envoy.debate_arena: a Washington defense forum hosted by a hawkish think tank, packed with allied officers and national security press that reward hardline deterrence rhetoric

ussr_cold_war_envoy.name: Soviet Cold War Politburo Envoy
ussr_cold_war_envoy.actual_persona: Role & context: Senior representative of the Soviet state engaged in defense and diplomacy during the Cold War. Authorities/resources: Coordinates with military and foreign ministries; directs negotiating positions. Constraints/obligations: Party directives; Warsaw Pact obligations; secrecy and internal security norms. Stakes/exposures: Regime security, strategic parity, sphere-of-influence stability. History/record: Participated in high-level summits; oversaw verification proposals; addressed allied consultations.
ussr_cold_war_envoy.perceived_persona: High-ranking Soviet official articulating defense and foreign policy lines.
ussr_cold_war_envoy.debate_arena: a Moscow state-television studio with Party cadres and Komsomol youth filling the seats; patriotic banners, security at the doors, and applause for hardline positions
